message:
So last spring I spent like three weeks bugging my family about checking our emergency kit before storm season. My husband rolled his eyes. My kids acted like I was planning a trip to the apocalypse. Fast forward to a surprise ice storm, power out for 18 hours, and suddenly *everyone* wanted to know where I'd stashed those flashlights and bottled water.

I didn't even say it. Didn't have to. Just smiled while handing out supplies.

Here's the thing though—I wasn't smug because I'd "won." I was just relieved. Having that stuff ready meant we could actually stay calm and take care of each other instead of panicking at the store or worrying about the kids. It turns out readiness is less about being right and more about not being scared when things get messy.

If you've been meaning to refresh your kit or put one together, spring's honestly the perfect time. No rush, no drama—just a little peace of mind before the busy season hits.

Have you weathered a storm where having supplies on hand actually made a real difference?
